plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet the concentration mandate and reduce overall risk.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with capital preservation.
Increase QQQI and QQQ to 25% each, favoring income and broad index for stability.
Add XLK at 5% for diversified quality tech exposure.
This plan significantly de-risks the portfolio by reducing leverage and concentration.
A cash buffer of approximately 15% will be maintained after these moves.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5% monthly return, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces downside risk by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration. Expect lower volatility, but also limited upside potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with concentration limits and reduce gross leverage.
Sell SOXL entirely due to being below stop and reducing overall leverage.
Trim SPAL to 5% to maintain a small, controlled leveraged ETF position.
Reduce QQQI to 10% as it was flagged for trimming, rebalancing the portfolio.
Increase SMH to 15% and buy XLK at 10% to add exposure to quality tech setups.
This plan balances growth potential with a focus on risk management and diversification.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for ~2.5% monthly growth by blending broad index with quality tech and controlled leverage. It reduces overall risk from the current state while maintaining growth potential. Expect moderate volatility and drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits while maintaining significant broad exposure.
Sell SOXL entirely to exit a position below its stop, freeing capital for stronger setups.
Reduce QQQI to 10% as it was flagged for trimming, optimizing for growth.
Increase SMH to 20% and buy XLK at 15% to lean into high-scoring semiconductor and tech setups.
Buy MRVL at 10% to add a high risk/reward setup, concentrating in strong trends.
This plan uses leverage through concentration in high-conviction setups, with a small cash buffer.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% monthly growth by concentrating in high-scoring tech and semiconductor setups. It embraces higher risk, including potential for significant drawdowns and daily decay from leveraged ETFs. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage amplified losses.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and free capital for income-focused assets.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with income strategy.
Reduce SMH and CRWD to 5% each to significantly cut high-beta single name exposure.
Increase QQQI to 40% to heavily favor income and covered-call strategies.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown over growth.
A cash buffer will be maintained to absorb minor fluctuations.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1% monthly yield by rotating into income and broad index ETFs, minimizing high-beta holdings. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income, but distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff. This is not a savings account.
